title: Counterexample Guided Program Repair Using Zero-Shot Learning and MaxSAT-based Fault Localization
reason: Combines LLMs with formal methods (MaxSAT fault localization) for program repair, following a CEGIS loop.

title: LLM+AL: Bridging Large Language Models and Action Languages for Complex Reasoning About Actions
reason: Bridges LLMs with symbolic action languages for complex reasoning, directly matching neuro-symbolic & structured reasoning themes.

title: SQLFixAgent: Towards Semantic-Accurate Text-to-SQL Parsing via Consistency-Enhanced Multi-Agent Collaboration
reason: Multi-agent LLM framework for detecting/repairing semantic SQL errors, directly addressing code reliability & correctness.

title: VerilogCoder: Autonomous Verilog Coding Agents with Graph-based Planning and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)-based Waveform Tracing Tool
reason: Autonomous AI agents for Verilog code generation, debugging, and verification using AST-based tools.

title: On the self-verification limitations of large language models on reasoning and planning tasks
reason: Directly studies LLM self-verification for reasoning/planning; shows external verification boosts code-like task correctness.

title: Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Collaboration Networks for Software Development
reason: Self-evolving LLM multi-agent system for software development, directly addressing code generation and correctness from requirements.

title: IRIS: LLM-Assisted Static Analysis for Detecting Security Vulnerabilities
reason: Neuro-symbolic approach combining LLMs with static analysis for security vulnerability detection (program reasoning).

title: ROUTE: Robust Multitask Tuning and Collaboration for Text-to-SQL
reason: Text-to-SQL via multitask tuning & agent collaboration to reduce hallucinations, directly improving code generation reliability.

title: SWE-Search: Enhancing Software Agents with Monte Carlo Tree Search and Iterative Refinement
reason: LLM software agent using MCTS for iterative refinement and self-improvement on code tasks (SWE-bench).

title: DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5: Harnessing Proof Assistant Feedback for Reinforcement Learning and Monte-Carlo Tree Search
reason: LLM agents for formal theorem proving using Lean proof assistant feedback, RL, and MCTS for proof search.

title: Active Task Disambiguation with LLMs
reason: Directly addresses ambiguity in NL specs for tasks, proposing active disambiguation via LLMs to improve correctness.

title: Agent Skill Acquisition for Large Language Models via CycleQD
reason: Method for LLM skill acquisition improves agent performance on coding tasks in AgentBench.

title: OpenHands: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents
reason: Infrastructure for AI agents that write code and solve software engineering tasks (SWE-Bench). Directly aligns with LLM-based agents for code.

title: Neural Interactive Proofs
reason: Neural interactive proofs for code validation with LLMs; directly combines formal verification (prover-verifier games) with code tasks.

title: LeanAgent: Lifelong Learning for Formal Theorem Proving
reason: LLM-based agent for formal theorem proving, uses structured reasoning (proofs) and lifelong learning for code/verification.

title: Robustness Auditing for Linear Regression: To Singularity and Beyond
reason: Directly benchmarks LLM-based agent security, including prompt injection and tool-use vulnerabilities, aligning with reliability for code agents.

title: Enhancing Language Model Agents using Diversity of Thoughts
reason: LLM agent framework (DoT) for code generation, reduces redundant reflections, improves pass@1 on programming benchmarks.

title: Diversity Empowers Intelligence: Integrating Expertise of Software Engineering Agents
reason: LLM agent framework for software engineering, directly improves code task success via multi-agent consensus.

title: CViT: Continuous Vision Transformer for Operator Learning
reason: Uses code verification and execution feedback to improve LLM instruction-following for correctness.

title: ImProver: Agent-Based Automated Proof Optimization
reason: LLM agent for automated proof optimization in Lean, uses symbolic reasoning and formal verification.

title: Training Language Models to Generate Quality Code with Program Analysis Feedback
reason: Uses program analysis (static analysis + unit tests) for RL-based LLM code generation, directly targeting code quality and reliability.

title: Self-Challenging Language Model Agents
reason: LLM agent framework for code-as-task generation, verification, and self-improvement via RL.

title: CodeCrash: Exposing LLM Fragility to Misleading Natural Language in Code Reasoning
reason: Directly studies LLM reliability for code reasoning under misleading NL specs, aligns with our focus on robustness and ambiguity.

title: SpecMAS: A Multi-Agent System for Self-Verifying System Generation via Formal Model Checking
reason: Multi-agent LLM system for formal verification, generates & corrects models via temporal logic model checking.

title: CLEVER: A Curated Benchmark for Formally Verified Code Generation
reason: Direct hit on formal verification + LLMs for code generation. CLEVER benchmark for end-to-end verified code.

title: Grammars of Formal Uncertainty: When to Trust LLMs in Automated Reasoning Tasks
reason: Uses SMT-based autoformalization & selective verification for LLM-generated formal artifacts, directly matching formal methods + reliability themes.

title: APOLLO: Automated LLM and Lean Collaboration for Advanced Formal Reasoning
reason: LLM agent (APOLLO) for formal theorem proving in Lean, using compiler-guided repair and sub-lemma isolation to improve correctness.

title: Wider or Deeper? Scaling LLM Inference-Time Compute with Adaptive Branching Tree Search
reason: AB-MCTS uses feedback-guided tree search for code generation, directly matching our focus on LLM reasoning and reliability for programming.

title: Agentic RL Scaling Law: Spontaneous Code Execution for Mathematical Problem Solving
reason: RL trains LLMs to spontaneously generate and execute code for math problems, directly aligning with tool-using agents for code correctness.

title: Diff-ICMH: Harmonizing Machine and Human Vision in Image Compression with Generative Prior
reason: LLM agent that self-generates code tasks and uses a code executor for verifiable feedback, improving reasoning without human data.

title: Can Dependencies Induced by LLM-Agent Workflows Be Trusted?
reason: LLM-agent workflow reliability with dynamic consistency checks and recursive reasoning for code tasks.

title: Co-PatcheR: Collaborative Software Patching with Component-specific Small Reasoning Models
reason: LLM-based multi-agent system for program repair, with specialized models for localization, generation, and validation.

title: Laurel: Unblocking Automated Verification with Large Language Models.
reason: LLMs generate assertions to unblock SMT-based program verifiers (Dafny), integrating formal verification with AI.

title: Active Learning for Neurosymbolic Program Synthesis.
reason: Active learning for neurosymbolic program synthesis; handles neural mispredictions via conformal evaluation for semantic correctness.

title: Large Language Model Powered Symbolic Execution.
reason: LLMs directly integrated with symbolic execution for program analysis, using path constraints and semantic reasoning.

title: AutoVerus: Automated Proof Generation for Rust Code.
reason: LLM agent network for automated proof generation in Rust verifier Verus, integrating formal verification.

title: ChopChop: A Programmable Framework for Semantically Constraining the Output of Language Models.
reason: LLM constrained decoding for semantic program properties (type safety, equivalence) via realizability analysis
